description: Merges HTSeq read count data files into one file
author: Broad Institute & Univeristy of California San Diego
plan:
## MergeHTSeqCounts GenePattern Module — Implementation Plan

### 1. Overview
MergeHTSeqCounts is a GenePattern module that Merges HTSeq read count data files into one file

### 2. Parameters
The module exposes 6 parameters:
- Required: input.files, output.prefix
- Optional: sampleinfo.file, filenames.column, class.division.column, sample.name.column

### 3. Command Line
```
<python_2.6> <libdir>mergeHTSeqCounts.py --input\=<input.files> --output\=<output.prefix> --sampleInfoFile\=<sampleinfo.file> --filenamesColumn\=<filenames.column> --classDivisionColumn\=<class.division.column> --sampleNameColumn\=<sample.name.column>
```

### 4. Docker Image
Docker image: 

### 5. Validation Strategy
- Validate all required parameters are provided
- Validate file formats match expected extensions
- Check parameter value ranges where applicable

### 6. Implementation Roadmap
Phase 1: Wrapper script implementation
Phase 2: Docker container setup
Phase 3: Parameter validation and error handling
Phase 4: Testing and documentation

parameters.name: input.files
parameters.description: A directory or one or more text files containing HTSeq read counts

parameters.name: output.prefix
parameters.description: The base name of the output file. File extensions will be added automatically.

parameters.name: sampleinfo.file
parameters.description: A sample info file containing HTSeq filenames and a column to base a cls file on

parameters.name: filenames.column
parameters.description: The column in the sample info file that contains the filenames.  This is used to match up the class division column up to the appropriate input file. This can be either a column index (starting at 0) or a string matching the header of a column in the sample info file.

parameters.name: class.division.column
parameters.description: The column in the sample info file that specifies a phenotype to use to assign classes and create a class file for the input samples. This is only relevant if a sample info file is provided.

parameters.name: sample.name.column
parameters.description: The column in the sample info file that specifies a sample name to use in the generated gct file.  If no sample info file is provided or no value is specified the columns in the gct file will match the file names of the input files.
